label: Heavy going
summary:
The Middle East still anchors the day on the downside. The US-Iran ceasefire is fraying again: Washington struck Iranian air defences and drones after accusing Tehran of downing a US MQ-1 over international waters, and the Revolutionary Guards say they hit back at a US-used air base. In Lebanon, Israel has pushed past the Litani toward the Zahrani, raised its flag over Beaufort Castle and now holds nearly a fifth of the country; the Lebanese health ministry counts 3,355 dead and more than a million displaced. Sudan's war grinds on in Kordofan with no peace in sight.

The economy leans the other way. US and Japanese equities sit at record highs — the Nikkei jumped 4.72% on the week — on hopes the Strait of Hormuz reopens and on the relentless AI cycle, and Kevin Warsh was sworn in as Fed chair. Goldman sees sturdy 2.8% global growth this year, though the ECB warns the Middle East energy shock is proving large and persistent. Climate is the harder counterweight: a record-shattering May heat dome killed at least seven people across Europe and pushed the UK to 34.8C, South Asia is enduring 46C-plus, and the WMO expects a developing El Niño to drive new heat records.

There is real light away from the front lines. Japanese researchers unveiled vitamin K-based compounds that may help regenerate lost neurons, and a Texas A&M nasal spray appears to reverse aspects of brain aging. Ethiopia held a general election with 50-million-plus registered voters, though observers call it a low-competition exercise with Tigray excluded. Kruger National Park marked its centenary. The headline lands at 45 — uneasy and mixed — held down by an escalating regional war and a brutal heat season, lifted by buoyant markets and steady scientific progress.

drivers.headline: US strikes Iranian air defences and drones after accusing Tehran of downing an MQ-1; IRGC says it hit back at a US-used air base
Israeli forces push past Lebanon's Litani toward the Zahrani, now occupying nearly a fifth of the country
Sudan's nearly three-year civil war intensifies in Kordofan with little hope of a peace deal
US and Japanese equities close at record highs on Hormuz-reopening hopes and AI momentum; Nikkei up 4.72% on the week
Goldman Sachs economists project sturdy 2.8% global growth in 2026, above consensus, led by a US rebound
ECB warns the Middle East energy price shock has been large and 'highly persistent', weighing on confidence and growth
Record-shattering May heat dome kills at least seven in Europe; UK hits hottest May day ever at 34.8C
Strong El Niño may stretch into 2028 as UN warns climate change will smash heat records over the next five years
Japanese researchers create vitamin K-based compounds that may help the brain regenerate lost neurons
Texas A&M nasal spray appears to reverse brain aging by calming inflammation and restoring energy systems
Ethiopia votes amid fragmented opposition and excluded Tigray, in what observers call a low-competition exercise
Kruger National Park marks 100 years as one of Africa's largest protected wildlife reserves
